name: Hardener
description: Cross-platform security auditing and hardening tool written in Go. Load a ruleset, run an audit, apply fixes, and roll back if needed — all from a single binary with no external dependencies. Supports Ubuntu, Debian, Rocky Linux, ArchLinux, RHEL, Fedora, and macOS. Published as ERNW Whitepaper #77.

name: Cherry Picker
description: Smart IP selection tool for penetration testers built during the CERN Whitehat Challenge. Leverages entropy filtering, TTL clustering, and cloud targeting for web enumeration & OSINT. Still in early development — the idea is solid, the time is scarce.

name: Forensic Log Tracker
description: Modular, secure CLI tool for forensic professionals and students. Implements cryptographic GPG signing and hash verification for legally consistent documentation. Used in university-level forensic training at Hochschule Mannheim.

name: ERNW Publications
description: Four security publications at ERNW: Unified Security Hardening with Cross-Platform Native Binaries (WP #77), the Hardener tool, Linux Client Hardening Guide (WP #76), and macOS Hardening Guide (WP #75, co-authored with Julian Suleder).
